messages.content: Write a forum convo in July 2008 about CW4Kids, when GoGoRiki will premiere, if Johnny Test and World of Quest are coming back, etc

**Thread Title: CW4Kids New Shows and Comebacks - Summer 2008 Buzz**

**AnimeFan88**
Posted: July 1, 2008
Hey everyone! Just saw the teaser on CW4Kids for GoGoRiki premiering this August. What do you all think? I’ve never heard of it before, but it looks like something different. Seems like it could be a fresh addition to the lineup. Also, any word on Johnny Test and World of Quest making a comeback?

**ToonAddict23**
Posted: July 1, 2008
GoGoRiki, huh? I'm intrigued! It’s originally called Kikoriki, a Russian animated series. It's pretty popular in several parts of the world. Super curious to see how it'll do here. As for Johnny Test and World of Quest, I’m dying for them to come back. Johnny Test has been a staple, and World of Quest was just starting to grow on me!

**MangaMaster507**
Posted: July 2, 2008
I heard GoGoRiki is quite educational but in a fun way. Always up for more diversity in our cartoons. Regarding Johnny Test, I read somewhere that there's a new season in the making. Fingers crossed it makes its way to CW4Kids. World of Quest deserves a second chance too. That show is underrated!

**SonicHedgehogFan**
Posted: July 3, 2008
I’m super excited for GoGoRiki! Looks like a good laugh. Also, YES, we need Johnny Test back! It’s just not Saturday mornings without him and Dukey’s crazy adventures. And totally agree about World of Quest. It was just getting good when it vanished. CW4Kids better not disappoint us!

**CartoonCritic91**
Posted: July 4, 2008
Gave GoGoRiki a quick Google, and it looks promising. I think it's great that CW4Kids is looking to bring in shows from around the globe. As for Johnny Test and World of Quest, my sources say that there are strong chances we'll be seeing more of them soon. Hopefully, we get official news in the coming weeks.

**RetroToonLover**
Posted: July 5, 2008
Holding out hope for more Johnny Test and especially World of Quest. Both shows have that unique mix of humor and adventure I love. As for GoGoRiki, it’s always exciting to see international shows make their way over. Let’s keep our fingers crossed and hope CW4Kids keeps delivering the goods!

**TooniverseExplorer**
Posted: July 6, 2008
Just jumping in to say I’m excited about all the potential new and returning shows. GoGoRiki has this cool vibe that could freshen up the lineup. And YES, we absolutely need more Johnny Test and World of Quest. Those cliffhangers can't be the end, right? Can't wait to see what’s in store for us this season!

**Thread Administrator**
Posted: July 7, 2008
Great discussion, everyone! We’ll keep this thread updated with any new information about CW4Kids’ lineup as soon as it becomes available. Keep the speculation and excitement going, and let’s hope for a summer filled with awesome cartoons!
Write a follow up with the CW4Kids lineup in early August, also a user says they pulled Test and Quest in late June

**Thread Title: CW4Kids Lineup Update & Johnny Test/World of Quest Pulled? - Early August 2008**

**Thread Administrator**
Posted: August 3, 2008
Good day, everyone! We’ve got a big update on the CW4Kids lineup for you all. As promised, GoGoRiki premiered yesterday, and it's been a colorful addition to the lineup, bringing its unique humor and storytelling from overseas. But that’s not all, we’ve got some updates regarding Johnny Test and World of Quest, and unfortunately, it might not be what many of you were hoping for. Let's dive into the details.

**CartoonWatchdog**
Posted: August 3, 2008
Just watched GoGoRiki's premiere, and it was quite entertaining! Love the art style and the quirky characters. A breath of fresh air for sure. But what’s this about Johnny Test and World of Quest? I’ve been waiting for news on their return.

**ToonAddict23**
Posted: August 3, 2008
So, I was channel surfing last night and noticed that both Johnny Test and World of Quest weren’t in their usual time slots. What gives? Those two were among my top favorites. CW4Kids hasn’t made any formal announcements, right?

**SonicHedgehogFan**
Posted: August 4, 2008
Heard from a reliable source they pulled Test and Quest in late June. Can't believe CW4Kids did this without at least giving us a heads-up. My Saturday mornings just won’t be the same. Any chance they’re coming back, or is this it?

**MangaMaster507**
Posted: August 4, 2008
This is such a bummer! Johnny Test and World of Quest had so much potential for more stories. It’s a shame CW4Kids didn’t try to promote them better or give them a decent chance. It seems like these decisions were quite sudden. Has anyone found any official reasons behind the decisions?

**RetroToonLover**
Posted: August 5, 2008
From what I could gather, it seems like ratings and perhaps a shift in programming direction might be behind Johnny Test and World of Quest being pulled. Networks make these changes, but it’s always tough for the fans. Let’s hope they’ll at least bring them to their website or something.

**Thread Administrator**
Posted: August 6, 2008
Thanks for sharing your thoughts and concerns, everyone. It seems the removal of Johnny Test and World of Quest was indeed quite abrupt, with limited information provided by CW4Kids. It’s unfortunate when beloved shows are pulled from the lineup, especially without closure. We’ll keep an eye out for any updates or shifts back in the programming that might bring these series back in some form. In the meantime, let’s give GoGoRiki a chance and keep supporting the shows we love. Stay tuned for more updates from the world of CW4Kids!
Write the August lineup, lasting from 7am to 12pm (3 KWB shows remain)

**CW4Kids August 2008 Lineup Update**

Good morning, CW4Kids fans! With the recent changes to our programming and the addition of new shows like GoGoRiki, we're excited to share our updated Saturday morning lineup, effective from this August. Mark your calendars and set those alarms because we've got a mix of new adventures and your continuous favorites from 7 AM to 12 PM. Please note that three slots continue to feature Kids' WB classics, keeping the spirit of our traditional animations alive. Here's what you can expect:

**7:00 AM - Will & Dewitt**
Start your morning off with the imaginative adventures of Will and his talking frog Dewitt. It's all about facing fears and finding fun solutions to daily challenges.

**7:30 AM - Magi-Nation**
Join Tony and his two Magi heroes, Edyn and Strag, on a mission to defeat the evil Shadow Magi. It's a magical journey in the Moonlands you won't want to miss.

**8:00 AM - Skunk Fu!**
The Valley is under threat, and only one unlikely hero can save it. Watch as Skunk learns Kung Fu and embarks on adventures to protect his homeland from Dragon.

**8:30 AM - GoGoRiki**
New to the lineup! Get ready to meet Pogoriki, Chikoriki, Rosariki, and the rest of the gang in this fun, educational animated series that's sure to bring laughs and valuable lessons.

**9:00 AM - The Spectacular Spider-Man**
Follow the web-slinging adventures of Peter Parker in The Spectacular Spider-Man. High school has never been more exciting with villains around every corner!

**9:30 AM - Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Back to the Sewer**
Dive back into the sewers with Leonardo, Michelangelo, Donatello, and Raphael. The turtles are on a mission to keep New York City safe from cyberspace threats.

**10:00 AM - Yu-Gi-Oh! GX**
Catch Jaden and his friends as they face off in the Duel Academy to become the King of Games. It’s all about strategy, courage, and friendship.

**10:30 AM - The Batman**
Explore the early years of the Dark Knight as he goes up against the criminals of Gotham. With a little help from his friends, Batman proves you don’t need superpowers to be a superhero.

**11:00 AM - (Kids' WB Classic) Pokémon: Diamond and Pearl Battle Dimension**
Continue the journey with Ash, Dawn, and Brock in the Sinnoh region. The Pokémon battles get even more exciting in the Diamond and Pearl series.

**11:30 AM - (Kids' WB Classic) Legion of Super Heroes**
In the 31st century, a group of young superheroes fights to save the universe. Join them as they battle evil in an action-packed adventure.

**12:00 PM - (Kids' WB Classic) Tom and Jerry Tales**
End your morning with a smile watching the classic cat-and-mouse chase. Tom and Jerry are up to their old antics in these new tales.

We hope you're as excited about the new lineup as we are! Remember, times are subject to change, so keep an eye on local listings. Join us for a mix of adventure, laughter, and lessons every Saturday morning on CW4Kids!
Write a forum convo saying the show Test was pulled for and the show Quest was pulled for

**Thread Title: "The Real Reason Behind 'Johnny Test' and 'World of Quest' Being Pulled"**

**ToonDude101**
Posted: August 10, 2008
Hey, fellow CW4Kids fans! So, I finally found out why 'Johnny Test' and 'World of Quest' were suddenly pulled from the lineup. Turns out, it was to make room for 'GoGoRiki' and 'Skunk Fu!' Thoughts?

**CartoonCritic91**
Posted: August 10, 2008
Seriously? 'GoGoRiki' for 'Johnny Test'? I mean, I have nothing against 'GoGoRiki,' it's a decent show, but 'Johnny Test' had a solid fanbase. Not to mention, it was mid-season. This decision doesn't sit right with me.

**WorldOfQuester**
Posted: August 11, 2008
And what about 'World of Quest'? That show had potential! 'Skunk Fu!' is okay, but it doesn’t fill the 'World of Quest'-sized hole in my Saturday mornings. I wish networks would consider viewer attachment before making these calls.

**SonicHedgehogFan**
Posted: August 11, 2008
I get the need for refreshing lineups, but completely pulling shows feels a bit drastic. Maybe they could have moved 'Johnny Test' and 'World of Quest' to a different time slot instead of outright removing them.

**MangaMaster507**
Posted: August 12, 2008
It's all about the ratings, unfortunately. 'GoGoRiki' and 'Skunk Fu!' must have been projected to do better. Still, it would've been nice if they'd given fans a proper conclusion to shows they suddenly decided to pull. Leaving us hanging isn't cool.

**AnimationAficionado**
Posted: August 13, 2008
I hope this isn't a sign of more abrupt changes to come. Part of growing a dedicated viewership is trust that favorite shows won't just vanish without warning. Networks should remember that.

**RetroToonLover**
Posted: August 14, 2008
It's a bummer, but at least now we know why the change happened. Maybe if we make enough noise, we can get them to release the unaired episodes or even bring the shows back in some capacity. Has anyone started a petition?

**TooniverseExplorer**
Posted: August 15, 2008
That's the spirit, RetroToonLover! A petition sounds like a great idea. Also, don't forget to express your thoughts on social media and directly to CW4Kids. They can't ignore us all. Here's to hoping for a miracle!

**Thread Administrator**
Posted: August 16, 2008
Great discussion, everyone. It's clear these shows meant a lot to many of you. Let's channel that passion positively by supporting the petition mentioned by RetroToonLover and voicing our opinions respectfully to CW4Kids. Maybe together, we can make a difference. Stay tuned for any updates or actions taken by the community!

Write the Fall 2008 lineup, starting September 13, Also a user says "Unfortunately time to add Johnny Test and World of Quest to the list of shows pulled without a proper ending, joining "Spider Riders" and "Monster Allergy""

**CW4Kids Fall 2008 Lineup - Starting September 13**

The CW4Kids is gearing up for an exciting Fall season with an all-new lineup that's sure to captivate audiences every Saturday morning! We've listened to our viewers and are bringing a mix of action, adventure, and fun that will keep you glued to your screens from 7 AM to 12 PM. While we're sad to say goodbye to a couple of our shows, we're thrilled to introduce new titles and bring back some familiar faces. Check out what we have in store for you this September:

**7:00 AM - Will & Dewitt**  
Start your day with the fun-filled adventures of Will and his talking frog, Dewitt. It's a show that proves friendship and creativity can solve almost anything.

**7:30 AM - Magi-Nation**  
Continue the journey through the Moonlands with Tony and his friends as they battle evil forces and uncover new mysteries in Magi-Nation.

**8:00 AM - Skunk Fu!**  
Watch as Skunk and his animal friends use the art of Kung Fu to protect their valley from Dragon and his minions. It's action-packed and full of laughs!

**8:30 AM - GoGoRiki**  
The gang from GoGoRiki is back with more hilarious and heartwarming stories. Join Pogoriki, Chikoriki, Rosariki, and the rest for endless fun.

**9:00 AM - The Spectacular Spider-Man**  
Swing into action with Peter Parker as The Spectacular Spider-Man. Facing villains and high school, Peter’s life is anything but ordinary.

**9:30 AM - Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Back to the Sewer**  
The turtles are diving back into the sewers and into trouble. Don't miss their new adventures in this action-packed season.

**10:00 AM - Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's**  
A new chapter begins with Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's. Get ready for more duels, more action, and more excitement as we take the Yu-Gi-Oh! series to the next level.

**10:30 AM - Chaotic: M’arrillian Invasion**  
The world of Perim is under threat again, and it’s up to the Chaotic players to stop the M’arrillian Invasion. Expect more battles, more strategy, and more chaos!

**11:00 AM - Dinosaur King**  
Join the D-Team as they travel the world to find dinosaur cards before the Alpha Gang can get their hands on them. It's a prehistoric adventure like no other!

**11:30 AM - Viva Piñata**  
Get ready to party with the piñatas on Piñata Island! Filled with fun dreams and vibrant adventures, it's a show that will bring a smile to your face.

**12:00 PM - Kirby: Right Back At Ya!** (Encore Presentation)  
End your morning with Kirby, the star warrior, as he battles to protect Dream Land from King Dedede's evil schemes.

---
**ForumUser1234**  
Posted: September 14, 2008  
Unfortunately, it's time to add Johnny Test and World of Quest to the list of shows pulled without a proper ending, joining "Spider Riders" and "Monster Allergy". It's always disappointing when shows we love don't get the conclusion they deserve.

---
It's important for us at CW4Kids to refresh our lineup with new content while also keeping fan favorites. We understand the disappointment that comes when shows are not renewed or concluded as hoped. However, we're committed to delivering quality programming that entertains and engages our viewers every Saturday morning. We hope you'll enjoy the new additions and returning classics in our Fall 2008 lineup. Thank you for being a part of the CW4Kids family!
Write that forum convo about JT being pulled without a ending, and also saying MN, W&D and SF are the last bastions of Kids WB

**Thread Title: "Johnny Test Pulled Without Proper Ending + The Last Bastions of Kids' WB: Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu!"**

**ToonFanatic42**  
Posted: September 15, 2008  
Absolutely gutted to see Johnny Test pulled from the lineup without any real conclusion. It's like a piece of my Saturday mornings just vanished without warning. And now with the new Fall 2008 lineup out, it's clear it’s not coming back. Also, can't help but notice that Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu! are the last shows holding the fort for what used to be Kids' WB. Feels like an end of an era is coming. Thoughts?

**RetroToonLover**  
Posted: September 15, 2008  
It's a real shame about Johnny Test. That show had so much more to offer. Pulling it without wrapping up the story just leaves all of us fans hanging. And you're right, noticing that Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu! are the last reminders of the Kids' WB legacy really hits home. Each of those shows has its unique charm and represent a piece of what made Saturday mornings great.

**CartoonCritic91**  
Posted: September 16, 2008  
Talk about Johnny Test being pulled is just heartbreaking. Shows deserve proper endings, especially ones with such a loyal following. As for Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu!, they're indeed the last bastions of what once was, and each of them holds up the spirit of Kids' WB in its own way. We should cherish them while they're still on air. Who knows what future programming changes might bring...

**AnimationAficionado**  
Posted: September 16, 2008   
I feel like we're witnessing the slow phasing out of what made Kids' WB iconic. First, we lose Johnny Test in a baffling move, now we're clinging to the few shows left that remind us of better days. Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu! each pay homage to the kind of imaginative storytelling and fun that Kids' WB championed. Let's not forget to support these shows and let the network know what they mean to us.

**WorldOfQuester**  
Posted: September 17, 2008  
As much as it hurts to lose Johnny Test the way we did, we can't overlook the treasures we still have. Magi-Nation is an underrated gem, Will & Dewitt teaches great values, and Skunk Fu! is hilarious while rocking some cool action sequences. They're the vestiges of a beloved era - maybe if we rally around them, we can show the powers-that-be that this kind of programming still has a passionate audience. 

**Thread Administrator**  
Posted: September 18, 2008  
Thank you all for your heartfelt contributions to this discussion. It's clear that the heart of Kids' WB lives on in its viewers and the passion you all bring to these forums. Let's continue to support Magi-Nation, Will & Dewitt, and Skunk Fu! as they carry the torch for quality, entertaining children's programming. And, who knows? Maybe our voices will be strong enough to bring about a change, or even a proper conclusion to some of our favorite shows. In the meantime, let's keep the conversation going and the memories alive.
Write a follow up about the other shows pulled without a proper ending over the years

**Thread Title: "In Memoriam: CW4Kids Shows Pulled Without Proper Endings Over the Years"**

**CartoonArchivist**  
Posted: September 25, 2008  
Since we've been discussing Johnny Test and the shock of it being pulled without a proper goodbye, it got me thinking about the other shows that shared a similar fate. Over the years, there've been a number of series under the CW4Kids and its predecessor, Kids' WB, that were left hanging, leaving fans with unresolved plots and unanswered questions. Let's take a moment to remember and appreciate these shows. Here’s a look back at some of those we've lost along the way.

**SpiderFan99**  
Posted: September 25, 2008  
Oh, the painful memories! "Spider Riders" is the first that comes to my mind. It had such an intriguing storyline, unique characters, and a world that was just beginning to unfold. It's disappointing we never got to see how the battle against the Invectids would've concluded.  

**MonsterAllergyZick**  
Posted: September 25, 2008  
"Monster Allergy" here! This show was my childhood! Zick had such cool powers, and the whole Monster Taming aspect could've gone in so many interesting directions. It deserved more seasons to explore the Monster World and Zick's destiny.

**QuestForEnding**  
Posted: September 26, 2008  
Don't forget about "World of Quest." It was such a great mix of humor and adventure, and it's a shame it got the axe just when the storyline was getting more interesting. I was really looking forward to seeing if Quest and the gang could ever find the Shattersoul Sword and save their kingdom from Katastrophe.

**LostToons**  
Posted: September 26, 2008  
It's such a bittersweet walk down memory lane. These shows, along with "Johnny Test," hold a special place in our hearts, not just for the entertainment they provided but also for the unanswered mysteries they left behind. They sparked our imaginations and left us dreaming of what could have been.

**NostalgiaNerd**  
Posted: September 27, 2008  
In a way, these shows pulling a disappearing act has made the community even stronger. We've come together to share theories, create fan fiction, and hold onto the hope that maybe one day, our favorite characters will return to finish their stories. Here's hoping that the future will be kinder to the shows we grow to love and that networks will ensure they're given the send-off they deserve.

**Thread Administrator**  
Posted: September 28, 2008  
This thread is a touching tribute to the series we've lost too soon. Let's continue to remember them fondly, celebrate their creativity, and keep their memories alive through fan discussions and creations. Who knows? With the ever-growing interest in reboots and revivals, we might just see some of these dearly missed shows make a triumphant return. Until then, let's keep the conversation going and our hopes alive.
Write a tweet in July saying Johnny is gone from August, replaced with a Russian toon, Quest is leaving so the Turtles can take over

**@CW4KidsUpdate**  
📢 Big changes coming this August to your Saturday mornings! Sadly, we're waving goodbye to Johnny Test 🚀💔 and making way for the hilarious adventures from Russia, GoGoRiki! 🌍😄 Also, we're bidding farewell to World of Quest 🗺️🛡️ as Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Back to the Sewer 🐢🍕 takes over their time slot! Stay tuned for epic tales and loads of laughs! #CW4Kids #SaturdayMorningToons #GoodbyeJohnny #HelloGoGoRiki #TMNTtakeover
Write a follow up forum convo asking why Johnny and not Skunk, saying Johnny is "with Zick, Omi, Ozzy, etc in Kids WB heaven"

**Thread Title: “Why Johnny and not Skunk? Johnny Joins Zick, Omi, Ozzy in Kids' WB Heaven”**

**CartoonLover101**  
Posted: July 30, 2008  
Just saw the tweet about Johnny Test being replaced by GoGoRiki and World of Quest making way for TMNT. I get change is part of the TV landscape, but why Johnny and not Skunk Fu? No disrespect to Skunk Fu fans, but Johnny Test had that unique blend of humor and action that’s hard to come by. Now, Johnny's up there with Zick from Monster Allergy, Omi from Xiaolin Showdown, Ozzy from Ozzy & Drix in what I call Kids' WB heaven. 😢

**SkunkFanatic**  
Posted: July 31, 2008  
I see where you’re coming from, but each show has its own charm. Skunk Fu has that unique blend of martial arts and comedy which isn’t common in western animation. It’s always a tough call for networks, and unfortunately, not everyone will agree with these decisions. Still, it’s sad to see any show leave, especially ones as beloved as Johnny Test. 

**RetroToonAdvocate**  
Posted: August 1, 2008  
It’s a tough pill to swallow. Johnny had so much more to give. Its cancellation feels premature, especially when it seemed like they still had plenty of stories to tell. Johnny Test now joins the ranks of other beloved Kids' WB characters like Zick, Omi, and Ozzy, who were taken from us too soon. This feels like a loss for creative storytelling in Saturday morning cartoons.

**AnimationHistorian**  
Posted: August 2, 2008  
It’s insightful to look back and see how many great shows Kids' WB and CW4Kids have given us over the years, only for them to be pulled just as they’ve hit their stride. The lineup decisions are likely based on a myriad of factors—ratings, demographics, licensing agreements—that most of us aren’t privy to. While it's disheartening, the legacy of these shows, including Johnny Test, will live on through their fans and online communities.

**NostalgiaCritic**  
Posted: August 3, 2008  
I remember waking up early on Saturdays just to catch Johnny and his antics. It’s a bummer that future audiences might not have that same chance. Johnny Test, Zick, Omi, Ozzy—all of them hold special places in our hearts. Maybe it’s time for a streaming service to pick these shows up and give them a new lease on life. They deserve to be seen by new generations and appreciated once again.

**ForumAdmin**  
Posted: August 4, 2008  
While it’s certainly endearing to see the community come together in support of Johnny Test and reminisce about the golden days of Kids' WB, let’s not forget that every end is a new beginning. GoGoRiki and the new season of TMNT might just surprise us and fill that Johnny-shaped hole in our hearts. Let’s give these shows a chance and keep supporting the world of animation. And remember, our favorite shows will always be available in reruns, DVDs, and perhaps even on streaming platforms in the future. Here’s to cherishing the old and embracing the new!
Write an ad that aired during the Johnny Test credits in July, saying about the show taking this time slot next week

[Scene: The end credits of "Johnny Test" are rolling on a blue background. The energetic theme song fades out, and suddenly the screen transitions to a vibrant, colorful graphic announcing the upcoming change.]

Voice-Over: [Upbeat, engaging voice] "Hey, CW4Kids fans! Brace yourselves for a brand-new adventure coming your way!"

[Cut to a montage of "GoGoRiki" clips showing Pogoriki and his friends in various funny and engaging scenes. The music is lively, with a catchy tune playing in the background.]

Voice-Over: "Next week, right at this timeslot, get ready to dive into the quirky world of 'GoGoRiki'! A place where laughter, fun, and wild adventures are just part of everyday life!"

[The screen showcases a close-up of Pogoriki smiling and winking at the camera, followed by Chikoriki performing a hilarious dance move.]

Voice-Over: "Join Pogoriki, Chikoriki, Rosariki, and the whole GoGoRiki gang as they take you on a rollercoaster ride of joy and mischief that you won’t want to miss!"

[A rapid sequence of clips shows the GoGoRiki characters engaging in various activities: a soccer game, a dance-off, and a comical science experiment gone slightly awry, all bursting with vibrant colors and energy.]

Voice-Over: "So, say goodbye to the ordinary, and hello to extraordinary! 'GoGoRiki' promises fun, laughter, and maybe even a little bit of learning along the way."

[The scene transitions to a graphic showing the show’s logo with the time and date of the premiere.]

Voice-Over: "Mark your calendars! 'GoGoRiki', taking over this timeslot starting next week, right here on CW4Kids. Be there or be square!"

[The ad ends with the GoGoRiki theme song snippet as the screen fades to the CW4Kids logo.]

Voice-Over: "CW4Kids, where the adventures never end! Stay tuned!"

[The screen fades to black, returning viewers to the regular programming schedule.]
